title: hieracium sylvaticum subsp. internatum
intro: Hieracium sylvaticum subsp. internatum, commonly known as the wood hawkweed, is a subspecies of perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family, with yellow or orange flowers and a bitter taste. The wood hawkweed has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Wood hawkweed is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal purposes, and its aerial parts are often used in herbal remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
